title: WHERE int_index_column > float64 disable partition and primary key index search
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
```
CREATE TABLE users_2 (uid Int16, name String, ts Int64,
INDEX ts_idx CAST(ts, 'Float64') TYPE minmax GRANULARITY 1000000
) ENGINE=MergeTree
PARTITION BY fromUnixTimestamp64Micro(ts) ORDER BY ts;
INSERT INTO users_2 VALUES (1, 'John', 33);
INSERT INTO users_2 VALUES (2, 'Ksenia', 1000000000000000);
INSERT INTO users_2 VALUES (3, 'Alice', 2000000000000000);
EXPLAIN indexes=1 SELECT * FROM users_2  WHERE ((`ts` >= 1700000000000000.) AND (`ts` <= _CAST(1800000000000000., 'Float64')));
Indexes:
MinMax
Condition: true
Parts: 3/3
Granules: 3/3
Partition
Condition: true
Parts: 3/3
Granules: 3/3
PrimaryKey
Condition: true
Parts: 3/3
Granules: 3/3
Skip
Name: ts_idx
Description: minmax GRANULARITY 1000000
Parts: 0/3
Granules: 0/3
```
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/771ddd1d-13d9-41dd-bba5-708fb890f399
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users_3 (uid INTEGER, name TEXT, ts REAL, age INTEGER);
SELECT * FROM users_3 WHERE ts > 1700000000000000.0 AND age IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: WHERE int_index_column > float64 disables index search
